SEARCH

Uses of Package
com.nib.commons.exceptions

Packages that use com.nib.commons.exceptions
Package
Description
com.nib.commons
Common utilities and core runtime components for the NIB application framework.
com.nib.commons.esql
API classes for Embedded SQL (E/SQL) integration and runtime support.
com.nib.commons.exceptions
Contains exception classes used throughout the NIB Commons library.
com.nib.commons.io
Provides core Input/Output (I/O) components and abstractions for handling various file organizations and access methods.
com.nib.commons.storage
Provides components for managing memory storage and simulating mainframe-like data types.
com.nib.scripting
NIB scripting framework.
com.nib.scripting.utils
NIB Batch utilities
com.nib.supernaut
Core implementation of the Supernaut online transaction processing (OLTP) region.
com.nib.supernaut.commands
CICS-like commands API classes.
com.nib.supernaut.exceptions
Exceptions for Supernaut online errors.
com.nib.supernaut.security
Security and authentication services for Supernaut.
com.nib.supernaut.systran
System transactions for Supernaut.
com.nib.supernaut.tcp
TCP/IP socket communication support for Supernaut.
com.nib.supernaut.terminal
General terminal control and emulation handling for Supernaut.
com.nib.utilities.api.le
Language Environment (LE) API utilities.
  • Classes in com.nib.commons.exceptions used by com.nib.commons
    Class
    Description
    ContextException
    Exception thrown to indicate an error related to program context.
    ExitProgramException
    Exception thrown to indicate an EXIT program.
    ExitSectionException
    An exception indicating an exit from a specific section of code.
    FlowException
    An exception representing a flow control change
    GoToException
    Exception thrown to indicate a jump to a labeled position in the program flow.
    ProgramNotFoundException
    An exception indicating that a program was not found.
  • Classes in com.nib.commons.exceptions used by com.nib.commons.esql

    Exception thrown to indicate an error related to program context.
